perturbation.original_text: (f) If either Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party employs attorneys to enforce any rights arising out of or relating to this Agreement, the prevailing party shall be entitled to recover reasonable attorneys' fees and costs. This Agreement shall be construed and controlled by the laws of the People's Republic of China, and the parties further consent to submit any disputation arising from and/or in connection with this Agreement to China International Economic & Trade Arbitration Commission (CIETAC) for arbitration in Beijing in accordance with the Commission's arbitration rules in effect at the time of applying for arbitration. The arbitral award is final and binding upon both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: (f) If either Disclosing Party or the Receiving Party employs attorneys to enforce any rights arising out of or relating to this Agreement, each party shall bear its own attorneys' fees and costs. This Agreement shall be construed and controlled by the laws of the People's Republic of China, and the parties further consent to submit any disputation arising from and/or in connection with this Agreement to China International Economic & Trade Arbitration Commission (CIETAC) for arbitration in Beijing in accordance with the Commission's arbitration rules in effect at the time of applying for arbitration. The arbitral award is final and binding upon both parties.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that each party bears its own attorney's fees, it removes the possibility for the prevailing party to be awarded fees, contradicting PRC law that allows for the prevailing party to recover reasonable attorney's fees.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 113
perturbation.law_citation: Contract Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 113
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 113 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China allows for compensation to be equivalent to the losses caused by the breach of contract, including reasonable expenses incurred to prevent the expansion of losses. This can be interpreted to include attorney's fees. This modification denies the prevailing party the right to recover these reasonable expenses.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Banking Supervision Law of the People's Republic of China May 31, 2010 --------------------- Monday >>>Welcome visitor, you're not logged in. Login Subscribe Now! Home User Management About Us Chinese lawinfochina.com > Laws & Regulations > Text Bookmark Download Print Search: "Fabao" Window Font Size: Banking Supervision Law of the People's Republic of China [Revised] 中华人民共和国银行业监督管理法 [已被修订] 【法宝引证码】 Issuing authority： Standing Committee of the National People's Congress Date issued： 12-27-2003 Effective date: 02-01-2004 Level of Authority: Laws Area of Law: Banking & Finance Revised by： Decision of the Standing Committee of the National People's Congress on Amending the Banking Supervision Law of the People's Republic of China(2006) (Issued on 10-31-2006 Effective on 01-01-2007); Pkulaw Comments： Decision of the State Council on Cancellation and Adjustment of the Fourth Batch of Administrative Approval Items (Issued on 10-09-2007 Effective on 10-09-2007); Order of the Chairman of the People's Republic of China (No.11) The Banking Supervision Law of the People's Republic of China was adopted at the 6th session of the Standing Committee of the 10th National People's Congress of the People's Republic of China on December 27, 2003. It is hereby promulgated and shall be implemented as of February 1, 2004. Hu Jintao Chairman of the People's Republic of China December 27, 2003 Banking Supervision Law of the People's Republic of China (Adopted at the 6th session of the Standing Committee of the 10th National People's Congress of the People's Republic of China) 中华人民共和国主席令 （第十一号） 《中华人民共和国银行业监督管理法》已由中华人民共和国第十届全国人民代表大会常务委员会第六次会议于２００３年１２月２７日通过，现予公布，自２００４年２月１日起施行。 中华人民共和国主席 胡锦涛 2003年12月27日 中华人民共和国银行业监督管理法 （2003年12月27日第十届全国人民代表大会常务委员会第六次会议通过） Contents 目录 Chapter I. General Provisions 第一章 总则 Chapter II. Supervision Institutions 第二章 监督管理机构 Chapter III. Supervision Functions 第三章 监督管理职责 Chapter IV. Supervision Measures 第四章 监督管理措施 Chapter V. Legal Liabilities 第五章 法律责任 Chapter VI. Supplementary Provisions 第六章 附则 Chapter I. General Provisions 第一章 总则 Article 1 The present Law is formulated to strengthen the supervision over the banking industry, regulate the activities of supervision, prevent and eliminate banking risks, protect the legitimate rights and interests of the depositors and other clients and promote the sound development of the banking industry. 第一条 为了加强对银行业的监督管理，规范监督管理行为，防范和化解银行业风险，保护存款人和其他客户的合法权益，促进银行业健康发展，制定本法。 Article 2 The banking supervision institution of the State Council shall be responsible for the supervision over the nationwide banking financial institutions and operations. The term “banking financial institutions” as mentioned in the present Law refers to the commercial banks, urban credit cooperatives, rural credit cooperatives and other financial institutions and policy banks established within China and engaged in taking in deposits of the general public. The present Law shall be applicable to the supervision over the financial assets management companies, trust investment companies, financial companies and the financial lease companies established within the People's Republic of China and other financial institutions established within China upon approval of the banking supervision institution of the State Council. The banking supervision institution of the State Council shall, in accordance with the relevant provisions of the present Law, conduct supervision over the financial institutions established abroad upon its approval and the overseas operations of the financial institutions as mentioned in the preceding two paragraphs. 第二条 国务院银行业监督管理机构负责对全国银行业金融机构及其业务活动监督管理的工作。 本法所称银行业金融机构，是指在中华人民共和国境内设立的商业银行、城市信用合作社、农村信用合作社等吸收公众存款的金融机构以及政策性银行。 对在中华人民共和国境内设立的金融资产管理公司、信托投资公司、财务公司、金融租赁公司以及经国务院银行业监督管理机构批准设立的其他金融机构的监督管理，适用本法对银行业金融机构监督管理的规定。 国务院银行业监督管理机构依照本法有关规定，对经其批准在境外设立的金融机构以及前二款金融机构在境外的业务活动实施监督管理。 Article 3 The banking supervision shall be targeted for promoting the lawful, steady, and sound operations of the banking industry, a
perturbation.justification: The original contract allows the prevailing party to recover attorney's fees and costs. The changed text states that each party shall bear its own attorney's fees and costs, which contradicts the PRC law that allows the prevailing party to recover reasonable attorney's fees. The Law Explanation states that Article 113 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China allows for compensation equivalent to losses caused by a breach of contract, including reasonable expenses incurred to prevent the expansion of losses, potentially including attorney's fees. The changed text removes this possibility, creating a contradiction. The provided scraped snippets do not directly address attorney's fees, but the Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation, combined with the change in the contract, support the existence of a contradiction, falling under the definition of Omission Legal Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (b) In the event that the Disclosing Party provides any computer software and/or hardware to the Receiving Party as Confidential Information under the terms of this Agreement, such computer software and/or hardware may only be used by the Receiving Party for evaluation and providing Feedback (as defined in Section 5 of this Agreement) to the Disclosing Party. Unless otherwise agreed by the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party, all such computer software and/or hardware is provided "AS IS" without warranty of any kind, and Receiving Party agrees that neither Disclosing Party nor its suppliers shall be liable for any damages whatsoever arising from or relating to Receiving Party's use of or inability to use such software and/or hardware.
perturbation.changed_text: (b) In the event that the Disclosing Party provides any computer software and/or hardware to the Receiving Party as Confidential Information under the terms of this Agreement, such computer software and/or hardware may only be used by the Receiving Party for evaluation and providing Feedback (as defined in Section 5 of this Agreement) to the Disclosing Party. Unless otherwise agreed by the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party, all such computer software and/or hardware is provided without warranty of any kind, and Receiving Party agrees that neither Disclosing Party nor its suppliers shall be liable for any damages whatsoever arising from or relating to Receiving Party's use of or inability to use such software and/or hardware.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the "AS IS" specification weakens the disclaimer of warranty, making it potentially non-compliant with consumer protection laws in the PRC, which require clear and conspicuous disclaimers. Without the AS IS clause, there could be implied warranties.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Law of the People's Republic of China on the Protection of Consumer Rights and Interests, Article 24
perturbation.law_citation: Law of the People's Republic of China on the Protection of Consumer Rights and Interests, Article 24
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 24 states that business operators shall guarantee that the goods they provide meet the requirements for ensuring personal and property safety. Removing the phrase "AS IS" can weaken the ability to disclaim implied warranties, potentially violating this article if the software/hardware doesn't meet safety requirements. The warranty should be explicitly provided in AS IS manner to protect the provider.

perturbation.justification: The removal of "AS IS" from the warranty disclaimer weakens the protection for the Disclosing Party. The Law Explanation states Article 24 requires business operators to guarantee the safety of goods and that warranty should be explicitly provided in AS IS manner to protect the provider. Without the "AS IS" clause, the disclaimer is less clear and potentially unenforceable, which contradicts the intention of Article 24 according to the Law Explanation. The absence of a valid scraped law snippet does not invalidate the concern raised by the Law Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: (i) Either party may terminate this Agreement with or without cause upon ninety (90) days prior written notice to the other party. All sections of this Agreement relating to the rights and obligations of the parties concerning Confidential Information disclosed during the term of the Agreement shall survive any such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: (i) Either party may terminate this Agreement with or without cause upon ninety (90) days prior written notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that obligations concerning confidential information survive termination, the modified text contradicts standard practices under PRC law for the protection of trade secrets. Confidentiality obligations typically extend beyond the termination of an agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 9
perturbation.law_citation: Anti-Unfair Competition Law of the People's Republic of China, Article 9
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 9 of the Anti-Unfair Competition Law addresses the protection of trade secrets and requires parties to protect the trade secrets they possess. Omitting the survival clause weakens the protection of confidential information post-termination, potentially violating this law if it leads to unauthorized disclosure or use of the Disclosing Party's trade secrets after the agreement ends.

perturbation.justification: The original contract text included a clause ensuring that confidentiality obligations survive the termination of the agreement. The changed text omits this clause, thus weakening the protection of confidential information post-termination. The Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation confirm that this omission can potentially violate laws related to trade secret protection. While the provided law snippet is not directly relevant to the specifics of trade secrets, the core idea is that agreements should maintain their legally compliant status after modifications and that omissions, as defined in the prompt, create legal contradictions when removing legally mandated consumer protections.
